steady.name: Steady
steady.description: Your duration stays exactly the same throughout the quest. Locked in. The most honest option for many practitioners — consistency, not escalation, is the path.
steady.suttaHint: The Buddha taught the same breath instructions to beginners and arahants. The depth changes; the form stays.

progressive.name: Adaptive & Progressive
progressive.description: Hit your current duration 7 days in a row, and the system offers a small bump (+3 to +5 minutes). You can always decline. Growth at the pace your practice can hold.
progressive.suttaHint: The Buddha called the path "gradual training" (anupubbasikkhā) — never sudden, always one supportable step at a time.

mixed.name: Mixed Days
mixed.description: Different days have different lengths. Perfect for an Uposatha rhythm: shorter weekday sits, longer practice on Sunday. You define the schedule.
mixed.suttaHint: The Uposatha days are themselves an ancient rhythm: ordinary days build the chain, the eighth and full-moon days deepen it.
